regulationSummary.overview: Ohio has no meaningful gold deposits, though glacially transported gold has occasionally been reported in Ohio stream gravels — carried from gold-bearing Canadian shield rocks during the Pleistocene ice ages. Quantities are far too small for productive prospecting. Ohio is well-known for flint (Flint Ridge State Memorial), fossils, and glacial minerals, but gold panning is not a viable recreational activity in the state. This entry is included for completeness.
keyRules: Ohio does not have a recreational gold prospecting permit system — there are no productive gold deposits in the state.
Rock and mineral collecting on Wayne National Forest land in southeastern Ohio is allowed for personal use in reasonable quantities.
In-stream work in Ohio waterways requires coordination with the Ohio EPA and Army Corps of Engineers.
Flint Ridge State Memorial prohibits removing Ohio flint from the site — collecting elsewhere on public land is generally allowed.
regulationSummary.hpaInfo: Not applicable — there are no gold-bearing streams in Ohio suitable for prospecting.
regulationSummary.suctionDredgeStatus: Not applicable — Ohio has no productive placer gold streams.
regulationSummary.handPanning: Gold panning in Ohio streams is not a viable activity — no productive placer gold deposits exist in the state. Glacially transported trace gold is theoretically possible in glacial outwash gravels, but quantities are too small to be detectable by recreational prospectors.
regulationSummary.disclaimer: Regulations change. Verify current rules at the official sources listed below before going out.

officialResources.title: Ohio Geological Survey
officialResources.description: State geological survey — Ohio has no gold-bearing geology; the survey covers limestone, coal, shale, and glacial deposits.

officialResources.title: Wayne National Forest
officialResources.description: USFS-managed land in southeastern Ohio — allows recreational collecting for personal use. Good for coal, fossils, and flint but not gold.
